commits: fix(skills): always show gotcha survey, let user decide on code-gen
decisions: Branched on questions[] array (the actual signal), not isReadyForCodeGen (the hint). questions being empty means there is literally nothing to survey. isReadyForCodeGen being true means the server thinks the design is high-quality, but questions[] is the true gate.

Three-branch logic: A (empty questions -> confirm to continue), B (non-empty + high grade -> ask review or skip), C (non-empty + low grade -> survey as before). This directly addresses the real user feedback: A-grade design auto-skipped survey the user wanted to see.

Removed the duplicate 'If questions is empty, skip to Step 6' guard at roundtrip Step 3 (old line 96). After Step 2 Branch A handles the empty-questions case exhaustively, this guard became dead code that would confuse future readers.

Updated the Edge Cases bullet in canicode-gotchas SKILL.md (old line 217) from 'The design is ready for code generation' to 'No gotchas surfaced' - keeping Edge Cases consistent with the new Step 2 Branch A wording.
No TypeScript source changes. survey-generator.ts already returns the full questions array regardless of grade - the issue was purely in SKILL prose.

ADR-016 compliance verified: the new branch conditions (questions is empty, isReadyForCodeGen boolean) are pre-computed server-side fields. ADR-016 explicitly permits 'branching on already-computed fields' in SKILL prose. The determinism checker confirmed no violations.
Followed the existing pattern in canicode-gotchas SKILL.md for prose structure: show grade, then branch on questions. The roundtrip SKILL.md mirrors the same pattern for consistency.
knownRisks: The roundtrip Step 1 description of isReadyForCodeGen still says 'boolean gate for gotcha skip' (line 62), which is now misleading. The plan did not ask to update this description, so it was left as-is to stay minimal - a follow-up could update it to 'boolean hint: true when the server believes the design is ready, but questions[] still determines the survey path'.

The 3 test failures (code-metrics.test.ts and html-postprocess.test.ts) are pre-existing infrastructure issues in the worktree caused by the dist/ directory not being built. They are unrelated to the SKILL.md prose changes and pass on the main branch.

Branch B (non-empty questions + isReadyForCodeGen=true) introduces an extra conversational turn before the survey in canicode-roundtrip. This is intentional but adds latency for A-grade designs that do have questions.
Wiki Decision Log entry is required per acceptance criteria but is not a file in this repo - it must be authored manually after PR review.
